batch

Class MetadataScanner

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.MetadataScanner
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>

    public class MetadataScanner
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>
    Facilitates the discovery and metadata extraction of media files within a directory tree.

    This class performs a deep scan of the file system using a FileVisitor. It validates media formats, extracts metadata via the ImageParserFactory, and maintains a sorted collection of MediaRecord objects based on chronological criteria.

    Progress updates are broadcast to registered ProgressListener instances during the scanning phase to allow for real-time UI feedback.

    Since:
    1 May 2026
    Version:
    1.2
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

        private final java.util.Set<MediaRecord> imageSet

    • Constructor Detail

      • MetadataScanner

        protected MetadataScanner(BatchConfiguration settings)
        Constructs a new scanner using the specified batch configuration.

        Initialises the internal imageSet with a custom comparator that handles chronological sorting (ascending or descending) and uses file paths as tie-breakers to ensure a stable sort order.

        Parameters:
        settings - the validated configuration containing source paths and sort preferences

    • Method Detail

      • iterator

        public java.util.Iterator<MediaRecord> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the discovered media records, ordered according to the configuration's sort preferences.
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>
        Returns:
        an Iterator for the sorted media record set

      • addProgressListener

        public void addProgressListener(progressbar.ProgressListener listener)
        Registers a progress listener to receive updates during file discovery.
        Parameters:
        listener - the listener to notify during the scanning process

      • start

        public final void start()
                 throws BatchErrorException
        Initiates the file system traversal to discover media and extract metadata.

        If a specific file set is defined in the configuration, only those files are targeted. Otherwise, the scanner performs a full recursive walk of the source directory.

        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if a critical I/O error occurs or the source directory is inaccessible

      • getRecordCount

        protected int getRecordCount()
        Returns the total number of valid media records identified during the scan.
        Returns:
        the size of the discovered media set

      • notifyListeners

        private void notifyListeners(int current)
        Broadcasts the current progress count to all registered listeners.
        Parameters:
        current - the current number of successfully identified media records

      • createImageVisitor

        private java.nio.file.FileVisitor<java.nio.file.Path> createImageVisitor()
                                                                  throws BatchErrorException
        Creates a FileVisitor instance designed to traverse the source directory for media discovery and metadata extraction.

        The returned visitor handles:

        • Filtering files based on user-defined file sets.
        • Detecting media formats and selecting the appropriate parser.
        • Building MediaRecord objects from extracted metadata and filesystem attributes.
        • Notifying progress listeners upon each successful record creation.
        Returns:
        a SimpleFileVisitor configured for the current batch scan
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if the source path in the configuration is not a valid directory
